title: larix gmelinii subsp. genhensis
intro: Larix gmelinii subsp. genhensis, also known as Dahurian larch, is a subspecies of the Larix gmelinii tree native to northeastern Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and rheumatism. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Larix gmelinii subsp. genhensis is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in topical applications such as creams and salves.
